message:
feat(fnp): port gcd/lcm from NumPy passthrough to native Rust

Lift fnp_python's `gcd` and `lcm` ufuncs off the NumPy passthrough
fast path onto the native fnp-ufunc Rust implementation, matching
the established pattern from heaviside (25904de), maximum/minimum
(a7a1f03), fmax/fmin (9319f49), and arctan2 (3162a81).

Why
---
gcd / lcm are integer-only element-wise ufuncs whose native Rust
implementation in fnp-ufunc (gcd_arrays / lcm_arrays at
crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs:30848 and :30877) is already shipping
and hardened. Routing fnp_python through them removes one more
hot dispatch from the NumPy round-trip and gives the operation
the same dtype/broadcast guarantees the rest of the native ufunc
suite enjoys.

Implementation
--------------
Add `gcd_arrays as ufunc_gcd, lcm_arrays as ufunc_lcm` to the
existing fnp_ufunc import group.

Add two thin wrappers — `native_binary_gcd_or_passthrough` and
`native_binary_lcm_or_passthrough` — that mirror the shape of the
prior native-binary wrappers in this file (`native_binary_minimum_
or_passthrough`, etc.). Each:

  - Falls through to `core_numpy_passthrough` if any keyword args
    are present or argv arity differs from 2 (preserves the
    out=, where=, casting=... etc. surface for callers who need
    them).
  - Otherwise extracts both args via `extract_numeric_array(...,
    "gcd(x1)")` / `"gcd(x2)"` so error messages still pinpoint
    the offending positional arg.
  - Dispatches to `ufunc_gcd` / `ufunc_lcm` with the
    `map_ufunc_error` adapter so any `UFuncError` is turned into
    a `PyErr` of the same Python-visible type the existing native
    ports raise.
  - Materialises the result back to a NumPy array via
    `build_numpy_array_from_ufunc`.

Repoint the `gcd` and `lcm` `#[pyfunction]` entry points
(crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:22662 and :22672) at these wrappers
instead of `core_numpy_passthrough`.

Tests
-----
New crates/fnp-python/tests/conformance_gcd_lcm.rs — 556 lines —
sets up a Python-driven differential oracle (the standard fnp_*
conformance harness pattern) and asserts native fnp.gcd / fnp.lcm
match `numpy.gcd` / `numpy.lcm` element-wise across:

- 1-D / 2-D / scalar inputs
- mixed positive/negative inputs (gcd is sign-agnostic, lcm is
  not — both pinned)
- broadcasting between rank-0 and rank-N
- int8 / int16 / int32 / int64 dtype lattice
- empty arrays
- gcd(0, 0) and lcm(0, 0) edge cases (NumPy returns 0)
- the keyword-arg fallback path (out= keeps passthrough behavior)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
